title: Chicano Prints
full_description: Radical Histories: Chicano Prints from the Smithsonian American Art Museum presents 60 works by more than 40 Chicano artists and collectives. Since the 1960s, Chicano artists have used printmaking as a vehicle for political advocacy, cross-cultural solidarity, and counter-history — resurrecting and memorialising underrepresented figures and events, and deploying text, poetry, graffiti, and historical quotation alongside image. Shown at H'ART Museum as part of American Identities, alongside the David Levinthal exhibition.
ai_summary: Sixty printworks by Chicano artists from the Smithsonian American Art Museum's collection — political, bold, and historically rich, tracing five decades of graphic counter-history-making from the Chicano civil rights movement to the present day, shown at H'ART Museum.
